mission.title: Install and Configure Grpc on Kubernetes
mission.description: gRPC is a modern, open-source high-performance remote procedure call (RPC) framework that enables client and server applications to communicate transparently. Installing gRPC allows you to build connected systems efficiently across various programming languages.

steps.title: Add the gRPC Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official gRPC Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration to access the gRPC installation chart.
```bash
helm repo add grpc https://charts.grpc.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install gRPC using Helm
steps.description:
Install gRPC in your Kubernetes cluster using Helm, specifying the version and creating a namespace if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install grpc grpc/grpc --version 1.78.1 --namespace grpc --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the gRPC pods are running successfully in the cluster.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace grpc
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the gRPC deployment to ensure it runs efficiently in your cluster.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment grpc --namespace grpc -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"grpc","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"512Mi"}}}]}}}}' 
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for local access
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the gRPC service to access it from your local machine.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/grpc --namespace grpc 50051:50051
```

resolution.summary: After completing these steps, you should have a running instance of gRPC in your Kubernetes cluster, with the pods successfully running and accessible via port forwarding. You can now start building your applications using gRPC.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add grpc https://charts.grpc.io
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed on your local machine.
